basic_details.job_title: AIESL Trainee Aircraft Maintenance Engineer Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 65 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: AI Engineering Services Limited - AIESL (A wholly owned subsidiary of AI Assets Holding Limited), New Delhi
basic_details.department: HR - HQ, AI Engineering Services Limited
basic_details.total_vacancies: 65 (Tentative; may vary depending upon Company requirements)
basic_details.short_information: AIESL is offering 65 Trainee Aircraft Maintenance Engineers (tentative) through an internal notice exclusively for serving AIESL employees, last date 26 May 2026 (5:00 PM).

important_dates.interview_date: To be notified by AIESL after closing date

application_fee.details: The notification does not mention any application fee for this internal recruitment. Candidates should refer to the official notification / Google Form on the AIESL website for any updates before applying.

age_limit.details: Upper age limit is 52 years as on 01-05-2026. Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/Ex-Servicemen categories.

qualification.education_qualification: Must have passed all B1 or B2 Modules of DGCA knowledge examinations; OR hold a B1 DGCA License or B2 DGCA License within AIESL capability. RT License would be preferred.
qualification.experience: Minimum 5 years of current practical maintenance experience on operating aircraft as prescribed under CAR 66, out of which at least 2 years must be in the services of AIESL.
qualification.details: This is an internal employment notice. Only serving employees of AIESL who meet all the prescribed eligibility conditions as on 01-05-2026 are eligible to apply. Candidates who had applied against previous notifications for Trainee - AME are required to apply afresh through the prescribed online application form.

salary_details.pay_scale: Existing pay of the selected candidate shall be protected during the period of training; salary protection given if trainee is already drawing a higher salary

selection_process: Selection will be based on assessment of eligibility criteria by a committee constituted for the purpose. No separate written examination has been announced. Final induction into the Trainee AME role is subject to successful completion of training

Candidates found suitable after assessment of the prescribed eligibility criteria by the duly constituted committee shall be considered for the post. Selected candidates will be designated as Trainee - Aircraft Maintenance Engineer till such time they acquire the DGCA license or equivalent qualification within AIESL capability.

Selected candidates will be imparted DGCA approved Type Rated B1/B2 training as per Company requirement. Candidates must execute a training bond as per the training policy before commencement of type training. On successful completion of approved training and after fulfilling the experience requirement
candidates must obtain the DGCA License or equivalent qualification. If a candidate fails to acquire the qualification within 4 years of joining as Trainee AME and being offered the type course
they will be reverted to their previous position and the bond amount will be recovered.
On acquiring the DGCA License in the appropriate category and on completion of the training period
the candidate will be placed in the applicable scale of pay. Allocation to Group A or Group B will be done only after all mandatory trainings are completed

depending on individual performance during training and existing vacancies in the respective group. Career progression will follow applicable AIESL policy as per the respective cadre (Permanent/FTE/AIXL/AASL).
how_to_apply: Only serving employees of AIESL who meet the prescribed qualifications as of 01-05-2026 may apply. Applications must be submitted online through the Google Form link available on the AIESL website. No other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates who had applied against any previous notification for Trainee AME must apply afresh. Applications received after the last date (1700 hours, 26-05-2026) will be rejected without any reference.
Apply Online (Google Form) at: https://forms.gle/P3KpXSsJGJ4dYNh59 (also accessible via AIESL website)
Management reserves the right to add, delete, or alter any of the criteria mentioned above and notify accordingly if necessitated.
important_instructions: THIS IS AN INTERNAL STAFF EMPLOYMENT NOTICE. Only serving employees of AIESL are eligible. External/open market candidates must not apply. Selected candidates will be designated as Trainee - Aircraft Maintenance Engineer till such time they acquire the DGCA License or equivalent qualification. They will continue to perform existing duties/functions and any other duties assigned in their present cadre during the training period. The existing pay of selected candidates will be protected during the period of training. If a trainee is already drawing a higher salary, salary protection will be provided as per AIESL Board approved policy. Selected candidates must execute a mandatory indemnity bond of Rs. 5,00,000/- (Rupees Five Lakhs) for serving AIESL for a minimum of 5 years from the date of joining or till superannuation/retirement, whichever is earlier. The bond amount is treated as liquidated damages in the event of premature resignation/separation and no pro-rata relaxation, partial waiver, or proportional deduction for early exit shall be permitted. In addition to the service bond, upon nomination for Type Training, trainees must execute a separate Type Training Bond prior to commencement of training as per Company training policy. If a candidate fails to acquire the DGCA qualification within 4 years of joining as Trainee AME and being offered the type course, they will be reverted to their previous position and the bond amount will be recovered. Allocation to Group A or Group B will be done only after all mandatory trainings are completed, based on individual performance during training and existing vacancies in the respective group. The employment status (Permanent/FTE) and parent vertical (AIESL/AIX/AASL) of the candidate will remain unchanged after acquisition of requisite qualification and absorption into the respective cadre. AASL candidates will remain in AASL; AIXL candidates will remain in AIXL, etc. Candidates can be posted to any AIESL station/region during training and subsequently at any base depending on Company requirement. No preference of posting location is guaranteed. Management reserves the right to add, delete, or alter any of the criteria mentioned in the notice and notify accordingly as necessitated.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for AIESL Trainee AME Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for online application submission through Google Form is 26 May 2026 at 5:00 PM (1700 hours) sharp. Applications received after this deadline will be rejected without any reference, irrespective of the reason.

faq.question: Q2. Who is eligible to apply for this AIESL Trainee AME recruitment?
faq.answer: This is an internal staff employment notice exclusively for serving employees of AIESL only. External candidates from the open market are NOT eligible to apply. Candidates must have minimum 5 years of current practical maintenance experience on operating aircraft as per CAR 66, with at least 2 years specifically in AIESL services.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are available in AIESL Trainee AME Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 65 tentative vacancies have been announced for Trainee - Aircraft Maintenance Engineer (B1/B2) posts. The actual number may vary depending upon Company requirements. A panel of suitable candidates will also be formed for future requirements.

faq.question: Q4. What are the DGCA qualification requirements for this post?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed all B1 or B2 Modules of DGCA knowledge examinations, OR hold a B1 DGCA License or B2 DGCA License within AIESL capability. RT License is preferred. This is a mandatory requirement.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for AIESL Trainee AME Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 52 years as on 01-05-2026. Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/Ex-Servicemen categories.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary during and after training?
faq.answer: During the training period, the existing pay of the selected candidate will be protected. If a trainee is already drawing a higher salary, salary protection will be given as per AIESL Board approved policy. After successful completion of training and acquisition of DGCA License, candidates will be placed in the applicable scale of pay as per AIESL policy.

faq.question: Q7. What is the duration of training for Trainee AME?
faq.answer: Training duration varies: For candidates already holding certifying staff authorization (B1/B2): 1 month; For those already holding Aircraft License within AIESL capability: on getting relevant authorization or 12 months, whichever is earlier; For others: 12 months.

faq.question: Q8. Is there any bond requirement after selection?
faq.answer: Yes, selected candidates must execute a mandatory indemnity bond of Rs. 5,00,000/- (Rupees Five Lakhs) for serving AIESL for a minimum of 5 years from the date of joining or till superannuation, whichever is earlier. Additionally, a separate Type Training Bond must be executed prior to commencement of Type Training as per Company training policy.
